headline: Chips skid, homebuilders soar as the money changes lanes
body_html:
Midday, the market is pulling in two directions at once. Beneath a firm tape — advancers beating decliners by better than two to one — a hard rotation is underway: out of the AI-and-semiconductor complex that has led for a year, and into the rate-sensitive corners left for dead. The 10-year Treasury yield has slipped back below 4.5% as oil eases, and that one move is doing the heavy lifting.

Nowhere is it clearer than in housing. Homebuilders are the standout, vaulting high-single to double digits after Congress advanced a bipartisan housing package and a major builder's quarterly revenue edged past expectations. Lower yields promise mortgage relief, and traders wasted no time repricing the group. Healthcare and biotech are catching a similar bid, with diagnostics names among the day's biggest gainers, as defensive growth comes back into fashion.

The mirror image is in chips. Memory and AI-infrastructure names are under heavy pressure — storage and packaging suppliers down sharply — as skepticism builds over whether hyperscaler AI budgets will earn their keep. Micron's results after the bell now loom as the session's real test: a soft outlook would harden the bear case on AI capex; a strong one could stem the bleeding. Into the close, watch whether the rotation holds.

briefs.headline: Builders break out
briefs.body: Homebuilders led the board, several up high-single to double digits, after Congress moved a bipartisan housing bill and a major builder topped quarterly revenue estimates. With the 10-year yield back under 4.5%, the market is betting on mortgage-rate relief — and repricing the most rate-sensitive group first.

briefs.headline: The AI trade wobbles
briefs.body: The semiconductor and memory complex sold off hard at midday as investors questioned whether hyperscalers' vast AI spending will earn the returns to justify it. Storage, packaging and chip-IP suppliers led the declines — a sharp reversal for the cohort that has carried the market for the past year.

briefs.headline: Yields ease as oil slips
briefs.body: A pullback in crude helped drag the 10-year Treasury yield below 4.5%, loosening financial conditions intraday. Lower yields are the connective tissue behind the day's moves — lifting long-duration, rate-sensitive equities like housing while doing little for a chip group nursing its own demand worries.

briefs.headline: Broad, not narrow
briefs.body: Beneath the headline index moves, breadth is firm: advancers are beating decliners by better than two to one across the large-cap tape. That marks a shift from a market long driven by a handful of megacap chips — a sign the buying is broadening even as the former leaders stumble.

briefs.headline: Defensive growth gets a bid
briefs.body: Healthcare is quietly having a strong session, with diagnostics and life-sciences names among the biggest gainers and pharma broadly green. As yields fall and the AI trade cools, money is rotating toward steadier earnings — a classic late-cycle tilt back to defensible growth.

briefs.headline: Micron is the night's test
briefs.body: Chipmaker Micron reports after the close and has become the session's marquee event. With memory stocks already pressured on AI-spending doubts, its guidance will set the tone — for the chip group and for an Asian session that has lately taken its cue from US semiconductor sentiment.
